title: MINOR 6 - Screening Test
readingMaterial:
# The Fascinating World of Cinema

Cinema is often called the "seventh art" because it combines many art forms such as literature, music, painting, photography, dance, and theatre. Since its invention in the late nineteenth century, cinema has become one of the most influential forms of entertainment and communication in the world.

The first public film screenings were held in the 1890s. These early films were very short and silent. Actors could not speak on screen, so stories were told through actions, facial expressions, and title cards. In 1927, the release of *The Jazz Singer* marked a major turning point because it was among the first successful films to include synchronized sound. This innovation transformed the film industry and changed audience expectations forever.

Films are usually classified into genres. Popular genres include action, comedy, drama, horror, science fiction, fantasy, romance, and documentary. Each genre follows certain conventions. For example, action films often feature fast-paced sequences and physical conflict, while science-fiction films explore futuristic technology, space travel, or imagined worlds.

Behind every film is a large team of creative professionals. The director is often considered the artistic leader of a film. Screenwriters create the story and dialogue. Cinematographers decide how scenes are filmed, while editors arrange the footage to create a smooth narrative. Music composers and sound designers help create emotional impact and atmosphere.

One of the most important elements of cinema is cinematography. Camera angles and shots influence how audiences feel about a scene. A close-up can reveal a character's emotions, while a long shot can show the surrounding environment. Lighting is equally important. Bright lighting may create a cheerful mood, whereas shadows can create suspense or mystery.

Special effects have always been part of cinema. Early filmmakers used simple tricks such as double exposure and miniature models. Modern films often use computer-generated imagery (CGI) to create realistic creatures, imaginary worlds, and spectacular action sequences. However, many filmmakers believe that technology should support storytelling rather than replace it.

Film awards celebrate excellence in cinema. One of the most famous international awards is the Academy Awards, popularly known as the Oscars. Many countries also have their own national film awards that recognize achievements in acting, directing, music, writing, and technical fields.

Cinema has a powerful cultural influence. Films can introduce audiences to different societies, languages, and traditions. Historical films help people learn about the past, while documentaries explore real-life events and issues. Many films also address social concerns such as equality, education, environmental protection, and human rights.

Today, audiences watch films in theatres, on television, and through online streaming platforms. Digital technology has made films more accessible than ever before. Despite these changes, the fundamental purpose of cinema remains the same: to tell engaging stories that entertain, inform, and inspire people around the world.

questions.text: Why is cinema often called the "seventh art"?
options: It is the seventh invention in history
It combines several art forms into one medium
It was invented in seven countries

questions.text: Early films were mainly?
options: Silent films with no spoken dialogue

questions.text: How did audiences understand stories in silent films?
options: Through actions, expressions, and title cards

questions.text: Why is The Jazz Singer important in film history?
options: It was among the first successful sound films

questions.text: A genre is best defined as?
options: A category of films with similar characteristics

questions.text: Which genre is most likely to explore futuristic technology?
The director is usually responsible for?
options: Leading the artistic vision of the film

questions.text: What is the primary role of a screenwriter?
Cinematographers are mainly concerned with?
Editors contribute to a film by?
options: Arranging footage into a coherent story

questions.text: A close-up shot is mainly used to?
A long shot is useful for showing?
Shadows in lighting often create?
According to the passage, special effects in early cinema included?
CGI stands for technology used to?
options: Create visual effects and digital imagery

questions.text: What caution do many filmmakers express about technology?
options: Technology should support storytelling, not replace it

questions.text: The Oscars are?
Film awards recognize excellence in?
Historical films can help audiences?
Documentaries primarily focus on?
According to the passage, films can introduce audiences to?
Which social issue mentioned in the passage may be explored through cinema?
What major change has digital technology brought to cinema?
options: It made films more accessible to audiences

questions.text: Which statement best reflects the author's view?
options: Cinema combines entertainment, education, and cultural influence
Technology is more important than stories
Awards are the purpose of filmmaking

questions.text: If a filmmaker wants viewers to understand a character's feelings, which technique from the passage would be most effective?
Which profession would be most responsible for creating a film's atmosphere through music?
Which statement best summarizes the relationship between technology and cinema?
options: Technology continually changes how films are made and viewed
Technology has no effect on cinema

questions.text: A student who understands the passage should recognize that cinema is?
options: A collaborative art form involving many specialists
A modern invention with no history

questions.text: The passage suggests that films remain important because they?
options: Tell stories that entertain, inform, and inspire people

questions.text: Which statement best captures the overall message of the reading?
options: Cinema has evolved through technology while continuing to influence culture and storytelling worldwide
Silent films are superior to modern films
Awards are the most important part of cinema
Technology is the only reason films are popular
